授業目的: Kwansei Gakuin was founded in 1889 by Rev. Walter Russell Lambuth, a missionary of the Southern Methodist Episcopal Church in the United States. Ever since its founding, Kwansei Gakuin has educated the young based on the principles of Christianity. Throughout its history, the introductory courses for Christianity have been at the heart of our education. The main purpose of “Christianity A and B” is therefore to provide students, specifically students with little or no exposure to Christianity, with the world view and the value system of Christianity through studying the Bible and the history of Christianity. Also this course explores the spirit and ideals of establishing of Kwansei Gakuin and its history.“An Introduction to Christianity B” focuses on the following topics in relation to Christianity: (1) Defining Religion, (2) Human Well being, (3) History, (4) Science, (5) Politics, (6) Culture, (7) Rituals.
到達目標: Students will acquire basic knowledge of (1) the role that faith plays in people lives, (2) a brief history of Christianity and its main traditions, (3) present day Christian belief in its relation to other disciplines.
授業方法: Course consists of: 1. lectures, 2. in-class discussions, 3. weekly short reflection essays, 4. excursion, 5. student presentations.

トピック.授業外学習2:
Students are expected to review of class material before the next class, complete reading assignments, participate in the excursion.
In order to gain a better understanding and appreciation of Christianity as a living tradition, students are strongly encourage to attend chapel once a week.
